result.result:
## Week of Apr 13–18 on agent-office

**63 commits, all on `main`.** Broad arc: built the thing from Phase 1 foundations up through a unified memory + persona-brief UX.

**Phase 1–4 (Apr 13):** Scaffolded the project from scratch — `bd59458` Phase 1 foundation, `6a1ede2` Phase 2 agent core (personas/skills/launcher), `ca066d7` Phase 3 memory engine, `eaebe7c` Phase 4 office UI with animated scene.

**Pixel engine extraction (Apr 14):** Pulled the pixel-agents renderer into reusable TS modules (`a88a3c6`, `54fcd13`, `00cdc7b`, `9df83eb`, `769983d`, `fd62f21`), wired it into React via `OfficeCanvas` (`c8c8f57`, `6ac04c0`), and added the 3-col dashboard (`68467b0`).

**Phase 4.5/4.6 — launch preview + claude-mem (Apr 14):** Read-only claude-mem SQLite adapter (`d109f26`), persona-filter heuristic (`e079b4f`), `LaunchPreview` component (`27cb1d3`), iTerm2 spawn (`64ed75e`). Merged as `6b61356`.

**Phase 5 telemetry (Apr 15):** `2534692` + merge `3199451`, then provider adapters / portfolio stats (`4d9806d`), launch picker redesign (`42aab52`), skill roots (`a4cb528`).

**Apr 16:** Provider-neutral history ingestion (`9c4afb4`), `doctor` command (`3b9f1a9`), live-session stability fixes (`a8f6bd4`, `aaa47ed`), Codex history enrichment (`fc03de3`).

**Apr 18 — unified memory + UX polish:** Persona brief generator (`4d9a58a`), bench harness for brief-vs-raw token savings (`40c5014`), Playwright LinkedIn carousel capture (`01f2a70`), simplified office floor to Boss/Workspace/Dungeon + Fixer (`d5da889`), README rewrite (`18e1cbb`). Then wired the brief into live launches (`17bf243`), rendered it in `LaunchPreview` (`ec56fb9`), exposed unified memory stats + semantic search and **dropped the claude-mem fallback** (`2dbef00`), surfaced those in the picker (`3c1cf94`), and consolidated the flow into one `LaunchWizard` modal (`3b6c0b4`).

**Uncommitted (today):** `bench/`, `scripts/bench-aggregate.mjs`, `scripts/bench-judge.mjs`, `scripts/bench-whitepaper.mjs` — whitepaper benchmark suite, per memory S291/3761–3778 this is in-flight and not yet committed.

**Key decision:** dropping the claude-mem fallback in `2dbef00` in favor of the native unified memory store — this is the load-bearing architecture shift of the week; everything after it (`3c1cf94`, `3b6c0b4`) builds on that assumption.

**Uncertain:** I can see the commit messages but haven't read the diffs, so I can't verify e.g. how much of "unified memory" is genuinely unified vs. renamed claude-mem code. The benchmark numbers referenced in the README rewrite (`18e1cbb`) aren't something I've verified — the `bench/` results are still uncommitted.
